name: @chbrain/khai-engine-hierarchy
description: khai engine content, the hierarchy domain: hierarchy as position, the place a structure assigns a persona in its formal order -- superior, peer, subordinate.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Hierarchy as position: the place a structure assigns a persona in its formal order -- who directs, who answers, who stands level.

card.wire: Attaches at Position. The anchor (position_hierarchy.md) declares the assigned place: a rank fixed by the structure before the scene -- who directs whom, who answers to whom, who stands level -- held whether or not the persona could re-earn it today. What the room reads from capital is the capital engine's; the deference order is the status engine's; this engine holds only the assignment.
card.issue: Three expressions a persona may hold, the title grammar any structure assembles from: superior (holds authority by the structure's rule), peer (equal rank, no line either way), subordinate (subject to another's authority by title). Each is a full position file the build composes onto the anchor; the same persona is superior in one structure and subordinate in the next.
card.require: Two altitudes hold the engine. The law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor, so assigned rank is read wherever a structure appears. The link, advisory: a persona standing inside a structure links its title position under Projection, and the audit surfaces the placed personas left unwired -- personas outside any structure carry no title, which is not a fault.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared and any linked expression is a real member. Whether the assignment is true to the play's structures, and whether the friction between title and the rest of standing is staged rather than ignored, is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite.
card.setup: Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Name the play's structures (the org, the chain of command, the family constitution), then give each persona inside one its title position under Projection. Cast the rest of standing from the neighbouring engines -- capital for the room's read, status for the deference order, power for the base -- and stage the disagreements between them; this engine holds only the assigned place.
